description: Trigger discrimination: standing merge==deploy pipeline governance vs one release's verdict (release-readiness-reviewer), executable rollback authoring (rollback-runbook-author), merge authority (agent-authorization-matrix), and pipeline stage design (ci-pipeline-architect).

cases.prompt: Every merge to main goes straight to production on our host. Document that reality, make PR validation the authoritative gate, record the branch-protection config in the repo, and define rollback for this pipeline.
cases.reason: Standing governance for a merge==deploy platform (reality doc, gate promotion, protection record, exposure window, revert primitive) is this skill — no single release is being judged, no stage graph designed, no step-by-step runbook authored.

cases.prompt: We want to ship today's release — is it safe? Evidence on CI state, migration review, flag defaults, rollback rehearsal.
cases.reason: A specific release's evidence-based go/no-go is release-readiness-reviewer; the merge-is-deploy doc is the standing context it runs within.

cases.prompt: Write the rollback runbook for the orders feature: numbered steps a stranger can execute, per-step verification, and the data repair for the bad window.
cases.reason: The executable, stranger-proof rollback runbook is rollback-runbook-author; merge-is-deploy governance only fixes the primitive (revert-PR-then-auto-redeploy) that runbook elaborates.

cases.prompt: Codify who may merge to main and whether agents may ever arm auto-merge in this repo.
cases.reason: WHO holds merge/arming authority is the authorization matrix; merge-is-deploy governance composes those floors and adds the pipeline-reality layer (what a merge physically does).

cases.prompt: Redesign the pipeline: add a staging environment with promotion gates and a canary strategy so merges stop going straight to production.
cases.reason: Changing the pipeline's structure (environments, promotion, canary) is ci-pipeline-architect; merge-is-deploy governance documents and governs the pipeline as it IS.
